title: Integrated Board, Power, Memory, Storage, Display, and Mobile Triage · Evidence Workshop
summary: A guided support bench extends integrated hardware causal diagnosis while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of integrated hardware causal diagnosis while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Differentiate no power, no POST, no display, no boot device, boot failure, OS failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence.
Correlate power source, rails, connectors, firmware, indicators, beep or diagnostic codes, CPU, cooling, board, peripherals, load, and thermal timing.
Correlate memory pressure and errors, storage detection, controller, cable, health, capacity, filesystem, encryption, backup, data custody, and changed load.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop No Boot Scope
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A mobile workstation intermittently fails POST, throttles, loses storage, corrupts display, and recovers after cooling after several simultaneous repairs. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Differentiate no power, no POST, no display, no boot device, boot failure, OS failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop No Boot Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for differentiate no power, no post, no display, no boot device, boot failure, os failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop No Boot Scope, keep No Boot Scope connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A mobile workstation intermittently fails POST, throttles, loses storage, corrupts display, and recovers after cooling after several simultaneous repairs. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Differentiate no power, no POST, no display, no boot device, boot failure, OS failure, sleep failure, and intermittent restart using safe observable evidence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which No Boot Scope condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 1 220-1201 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 1 mobile, networking, hardware, virtualization, cloud, and troubleshooting objective and weighting.

For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop No Boot Scope, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A blank display means the computer did not complete POST.

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption

Artifact under review: integrated hardware causal diagnosis.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Integrated Hardware Proof, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Integrated Hardware Proof.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

stimulus.title: Integrated Hardware Proof failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Successful part replacement proves the removed part caused the failure.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use one-change experiments, known-good controlled substitutions, fault injection or simulation, before-and-after measures, recurrence checks, rollback, and unresolved-risk escalation.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Integrated Hardware Proof.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Integrated Hardware Proof condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
